verses.t: and compassionate. And He setteth not down in account evil to the sons of men, because they are flesh, And the ruchot of deceit deceive them in all their deeds. 8 And after these things there shall arise unto you Yahuah Himself, the light of righteousness, [And healing and compassion shall be in His wings. He shall redeem all the captivity of the sons of men from Beliar; And every ruach of deceit shall be trodden down]; And he shall bring back all the Gentiles into zeal for Him. And ye shall return unto your land. And ye shall see Him in Yerushalayim, for His name's sake. 9 And again through the wickedness of your works shall ye provoke Him to anger, And ye shall be cast away by Him unto the time of consummation. 10 1 And now, my children, grieve not that I am dying, nor be cast down in that I am coming to my 2 end. For I shall rise again in the midst of you, as a ruler in the midst of his sons; and I shall rejoice in the midst of my tribe, as many as shall keep the law of Yahuah, and the mitsvot 3 of Zebulun their father. But upon the ungodly shall Yahuah bring eternal fire, and destroy them 4, 5 throughout all generations. But I am now hastening away to my rest, as did also my fathers. But 6 do ye fear Yahuah our Aluahim with all your strength all the days of your life. And when he had 7 said these things he fell asleep, at a good old age. And his sons laid him in a wooden coffin. And afterwards they carried him up and buried him in Hebron, with his fathers. 1 1 The copy of the words of Dan, which he spake to his sons in his last days, in the hundred and 2 twenty-fifth year of his life. For he called together his family, and said: Hearken to my words, ye 3 sons of Dan; and give heed to the words of your father. I have proved in my heart, and in my whole life, that truth with just dealing is good and well pleasing to Aluahim, and that lying and anger 4 are evil, because they teach man all wickedness. I confess, therefore, this day to you, my children, 5 that in my heart I resolved on the death of Yoseph my brother, the true and good man. [And 6 I rejoiced that he was sold, because his father loved him more than us.] For the ruach of jealousy 7 and vainglory said to me: Thou thyself also art his son. And one of the ruchot of Beliar stirred me up, saying: Take this sword, and with it slay Yoseph: so shall thy father love thee when he is dead. 8 Now this is the ruach of anger that persuaded me to crush Yoseph as a leopard crusheth a kid. 9 But the Aluahim of my fathers did not suffer him to fall into my hands, so that I should find him alone and slay him, and cause a second tribe to be destroyed in Yasharal. 2 1 And now, my children, behold I am dying, and I tell you of a truth, that unless ye keep yourselves from the ruach of lying and of anger, and love truth and longsuffering, ye shall perish. 2 For anger is blindness, and does not suffer one to see the face of any man with truth. 3 For though it be a father or a mother, he behaveth towards them as enemies; though it be a brother, he knoweth him not; though it be a naḇi of Yahuah, he disobeyeth him; though 4 a righteous man, he regardeth him not; though a friend, he doth not acknowledge him. For the ruach of anger encompasseth him with the net of deceit, and blindeth his eyes, and through lying 5 darkeneth his mind, and giveth him its own peculiar vision. And wherewith encompasseth it his eyes? With hatred of heart, so as to be envious of his brother. 3 1 For anger is an evil thing, my children, for it troubleth even the nephesh itself. And the body of the angry man it maketh its own, and over his nephesh it getteth the mastery, and 3 it bestoweth upon the body power that it may work all iniquity. And when the body does all 4 these things, the nephesh justifieth what is done, since it seeth not aright. Therefore he that is wrathful, if he be a mighty man, hath a threefold power in his anger: one by the help of his servants; and a second by his wealth, whereby he persuadeth and overcometh wrongfully; and thirdly, having his 5 own natural power he worketh thereby the evil. And though the wrathful man be weak, yet hath 6 he a power twofold of that which is by nature; for wrath ever aideth such in lawlessness. This ruach goeth always with lying at the right hand of Satan, that with cruelty and lying his works may be wrought. 4 1, 2 Understand ye, therefore, the power of wrath, that it is vain. For it first of all giveth provocation by word; then by deeds it strengtheneth him who is angry, and with sharp losses disturbeth his 3 mind, and so stirreth up with great wrath his nephesh. Therefore, when any one speaketh against you, be not ye moved to anger, [and if any man praiseth you as holy men, be not uplifted: be not moved 4 either to delight or to disgust]. For first it pleaseth the hearing, and so maketh the mind keen to perceive the grounds for provocation; and then being enraged, he thinketh that he is justly angry 5 If ye fall into any loss or ruin, my children, be not afflicted; for this very ruach maketh (a man) 6 desire that which is perishable, in order that he may be enraged through the affliction. And if ye suffer loss voluntarily, or involuntarily, be not vexed; for from vexation ariseth wrath with lying. 7 Moreover, a twofold mischief is wrath with lying; and they assist one another in order to disturb the heart; and when the nephesh is continually disturbed, Yahuah departeth from it, and Beliar ruleth over it. 5 1 Observe, therefore, my children, the mitsvot of Yahuah, And keep His law; Depart from wrath, And hate lying, That Yahuah may dwell among you, And Beliar may flee from you. 2 Speak truth each one with his neighbour. So shall ye not fall into wrath and confusion; But ye shall be in shalom, having the Aluahim of shalom, So shall no war prevail over you. 3 Love Yahuah through all your life, And one another with a true heart. 4 I know that in the last days ye shall depart from Yahuah, And ye shall provoke Lewi unto anger, And fight against Yahuḏah; But ye shall not prevail against them, For an mal'ak of Yahuah shall guide them both; For by them shall Yasharal stand. 5 And whensoever ye depart from Yahuah, ye shall walk in all evil and work the abominations of the Gentiles, going a-whoring after women of the lawless ones, while with all wickedness the ruchot 6 of wickedness work in you. [For I have read in the book of Ḥanoḵ, the righteous, that your prince is Satan, and that all the ruchot of wickedness and pride will conspire to attend constantly on the sons of Lewi, to cause them to sin before Yahuah. 7 And my sons will draw near to Lewi. And sin with them in all things; And the sons of Yahuḏah will be covetous, Plundering other men's goods like lions.] 8 Therefore shall ye be led away [with them] into captivity, And there shall ye receive all the plagues of Mitsrayim, And all the evils of the Gentiles. 9 And so when ye return to Yahuah ye shall obtain mercy, And He shall bring you into His sanctuary, And He shall give you shalom. 10 And there shall arise unto you from the tribe of [Yahuḏah and of] Lewi the yasha of Yahuah; And he shall make war against Beliar. And execute an everlasting vengeance on our enemies; 11 And the captivity shall he take from Beliar [the nepheshoth of the saints], And turn disobedient hearts unto Yahuah, And give to them that call upon him eternal shalom. 12 And the saints shall rest in Eden, And in the New Yerushalayim shall the righteous rejoice, And it shall be unto the glory of Aluahim for ever. 13 And no longer shall Yerushalayim endure desolation, Nor Yasharal be led captive; For Yahuah shall be in the midst of it [living amongst men], And the Holy One of Yasharal shall reign over it [in humility and in poverty; and he who believeth on Him shall reign amongst men in truth]. 6 1, 2 And now, fear Yahuah, my children, and beware of Satan and his ruchot. Draw near unto Aluahim and unto the mal'ak that intercedeth for you, for he is a mediator between Aluahim and man, and for the 3 shalom of Yasharal he shall stand up against the kingdom of the enemy. Therefore is the enemy eager 4 to destroy all that call upon Yahuah. For he knoweth that upon the day on which Yasharal shall 5 repent, the kingdom of the enemy shall be brought to an end. For the very mal'ak of shalom shall 6 strengthen Yasharal, that it fall not into the extremity of evil. And it shall be in the time of the lawlessness of Yasharal, that Yahuah will not depart from them, but will transform them into a nation 7 that doeth His will, for none of the mal'akim will be equal unto him. And His name shall be in every place in Yasharal, and among the Gentiles. 8 Keep, therefore, yourselves, my children, from every evil work, And cast away wrath and all lying, And love truth and long-suffering. 9 And the things which ye have heard from your father, do ye also impart to your children [that the Saviour of the Gentiles may receive you; for he is true and long-suffering, meek and lowly, and 10 teacheth by his works the law of Aluahim]. Depart, therefore, from all unrighteousness, and cleave unto the righteousness of Aluahim, and your race will be saved for ever. And bury me near my fathers. 7 1, 2 And when he had said these things he kissed them, and fell asleep at a good old age. And his sons buried him, And after that they carried up his bones, and placed them near Aḇraham, and 3 Yitsḥaq, and Ya'aqoḇ. [Nevertheless, Dan prophesied unto them that they should forget their Aluahim, and should be alienated from the land of their inheritance and from the race of Yasharal, and from the family of their seed.]
